what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet that defines the visual styling, layout, and interactive states for the Imagery subsystem of the ConceptV design system, covering asset grids, project/hub panos, a 360 viewer, an AI Studio generation interface, a mask editor, and an image editor modal.
observations: The file is organized by commented sections: pills/banners, tabs, toolbar/tag bar, image grid, empty state, projects/hubs layout, pano strip/card/hotspots, 360 viewer, AI Studio v3, mask editor, and image editor modal.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties such as --paper, --ink, --accent-gold, --s-*, --r-*, and --font-* for theming and spacing.
Uses modern CSS features including aspect-ratio, color-mix(in oklch, ...), backdrop-filter, sticky positioning, CSS grid, flexbox, and keyframe animations.
Class names are mostly BEM-like with subsystem prefixes: cv-im- (Imagery), cv-as- (AI Studio), cv-mask- (Mask editor), and cv-ed- (Image editor).
Interactive states are consistently styled with transitions on color, background, border-color, box-shadow, and transform.
The image editor modal includes a crop overlay with draggable corner handles and a rule-of-thirds grid.
The 360 viewer includes a pulsing gold ring animation for hotspot pins.
Only one media query appears, switching AI Studio results to two columns at 1200px.
Child class names like .dot, .lbl, .ct, .meta, .ok, .warn are reused under many parent selectors.
standouts: The large AI Studio v3 'deck-restraint redesign' section with presets, pipelines, parameters, runbar, results, chat, and modal.
The image editor modal with crop mask, dashed gold crop box, resize handles, and rule-of-thirds grid overlay.
The 360 viewer hotspot pin with a pulsing ring animation.
The warm-paper-with-gold-ink palette applied consistently across disparate subsystems.
conventions: BEM-ish naming with a two-letter subsystem prefix and double-hyphen modifiers.
Token-driven spacing, radius, color, typography, and shadow values.
Dashed gold lines used as section separators.
Uppercase, letter-spaced labels for form controls and parameters.
Hover and active states enhanced with transitions.
Grid and flex used for most layout structures.
concerns: The file bundles styling for several large subsystems (Imagery, AI Studio, Mask Editor, Image Editor) in one place, suggesting tight coupling.
Generic child class names (.dot, .lbl, .ct, .meta, .ok, .warn) are scoped only by parent selectors and could collide if reused elsewhere.
Minimal responsive design: only one media query and fixed widths/heights in many places.
Use of backdrop-filter and color-mix may have browser-support implications.
No visible accessibility features such as focus-visible styles, reduced-motion support, or ARIA-related selectors.
notes: A dense, token-driven stylesheet that reads like a UI component catalog for the Imagery surface and its integrated AI, panorama, masking, and editing tools.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties (--paper, --accent-gold, --s-1, etc.) defined?
What JavaScript components drive the 360 viewer, mask canvas, crop handles, and AI Studio pipelines?
Are the AI Studio, mask editor, and image editor intended to remain in this file or be split into separate stylesheets?
How are keyboard focus and accessibility handled for the crop handles, hotspot pins, and toolbar buttons?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles the Imagery subsystem, settings, editor, and 360 viewer. It also includes AI Studio v3 and a mask editor, which is slightly broader than the opening comment suggests but still imagery-related.
apparent_themes: Warm editorial/studio aesthetic using paper, gold, and bronze tones.
Progressive disclosure through hover overlays, modals, and collapsible panels.
Integration of traditional asset management with AI-assisted generation and editing.
intention_signals: To provide a polished, cohesive visual system for browsing, organizing, editing, and generating images within a design-studio application, emphasizing warmth, precision, and AI-assisted workflows.
novelty: The unusual combination of a traditional imagery asset manager, an AI generation studio, a mask editor, and a full image editor modal within a single warm-paper/gold-ink aesthetic; the crop overlay with rule-of-thirds grid and animated 360 hotspot pins are distinctive details.
contribution: A comprehensive set of styled UI patterns for imagery grids, project/hub panos, 360 panoramas, AI image generation, mask editing, and image editing, all expressed through a shared warm-paper-with-gold-ink token system; it contributes concrete component-level styling that a fused system could reuse for these surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet for the ConceptV Imagery subsystem that styles asset grids, project/hub panos, a 360 viewer, an AI Studio generation interface, a mask editor, and an image editor modal using a warm-paper-with-gold-ink design-token palette.
intention_for_embedding: To define the visual and interactive styling for the Imagery section and its integrated AI, panorama, masking, and editing tools in the ConceptV design system.
